title: Crossroads of Competition: China in Southeast Asia and the Pacific Islands
description: The hearing examines how Southeast Asia and the Pacific Islands factor into Chinese strategic thinking and China's diplomatic and soft power initiatives in both regions, reviews China's role in Southeast Asian and Pacific Islands security issues, and explores China's economic engagement.
text:
AGENDA
Hearing on "Crossroads of Competition: China in Southeast Asia and the Pacific Islands"
Thursday, March 20, 2025
Dirksen Senate Office Building, Room 215, and Webex
Unable to see this video? Click here.
Hearing Co-Chairs: Chair Reva Price and Vice Chair Randall Schriver
9:30 AM – 9:40 AM: Co-Chairs' Opening Remarks
9:40 AM – 11:10 AM: Panel I: U.S.-China Strategic Competition in Southeast Asia and the Pacific Islands
Prashanth Parameswaran, Global Fellow, Wilson Center [Testimony]
Lynn Kuok, Lee Kuan Yew Chair in Southeast Asia Studies, Brookings Institution [Testimony]
Graeme Smith, Senior Fellow, Australian National University [Testimony]
11:10 AM – 11:20 AM: Break
11:20 AM – 12:50 PM: Panel II: China’s Military and Security Footprint in Southeast Asia and the Pacific Islands
Gregory B. Poling, Director and Senior Fellow, Southeast Asia Program and Asia Maritime Transparency Initiative, Center for Strategic and International Studies [Testimony]
Jason Tower, Country Director for Burma, United States Institute of Peace [Testimony]
Cleo Paskal, Non-resident Senior Fellow, Foundation for Defense of Democracies [Testimony]
12:50 PM – 1:50 PM: Lunch Break
1:50 PM – 3:20 PM: Panel III: Economic Risks and Rewards at the Crossroads
Vikram Nehru, Senior Fellow, Foreign Policy Institute, Johns Hopkins University School of Advanced International Studies [Testimony]
Alvin Camba, Critical Materials Specialist, Associated Universities Incorporated [Testimony]
Guanie Lim, Associate Professor, National Graduate Institute for Policy Studies [Testimony]
3:20 PM – 3:25 PM: Closing Remarks
